old: 他在这个环境中发挥了自己的能力。
new: 这次比赛让他充分发挥了自己的能力。
reason: All three sentences use 发挥了自己的能力 — near-duplicates. Replace sentence_1 with a distinct scenario.

old: He was able to display his abilities in this environment.
new: The competition allowed him to fully bring his abilities into play.

old: 孩子在好的教育下发挥了自己的能力。
new: 好的老师能帮学生发挥潜力。
reason: Near-duplicate of sentences 1 and 3 (all use 发挥了自己的能力). Replace with distinct scenario.

old: Children can display their abilities under good education.
new: A good teacher can help students bring out their potential.

old: 我以为他会高兴，反倒他不高兴。
new: 我以为他会高兴，反他却不高兴。
reason: Uses 反倒 (a separate compound) rather than the headword 反 as standalone adverb meaning 'on the contrary'.

new: 这种药能防止感冒，但防不了所有的病。
reason: Sentence_1 and sentence_2 are near-identical (both: exercising more prevents illness). Replace sentence_2 with a distinct scenario.

new: This medicine can prevent colds but cannot prevent all illnesses.
one after another; in rapid succession
reason: All three example sentences and translations illustrate 'one after another / in quick succession' — the primary adverbial meaning of 纷纷. 'Numerous and disorderly' does not match.
丰富多彩 is an adjective, not a verb.
Definition is 'to negate'; 'denied' implies refuting something true, while 否定 means to reject/negate validity.
Consistent correction: 'negated' better matches definition 'to negate'.

old: You cannot easily deny others' efforts.
new: You cannot easily negate others' efforts.

old: He paid a lot for his work.
new: He put a great deal into his work.
reason: 付出 means to expend/devote effort, not to pay money. 'Paid a lot for' implies financial payment.
